title: Agentic protocol
description: Schema for a protocol.json — the data declaration the generic engine interprets. Authoring/editor aid: it is intentionally STRICTER than the engine (which ignores unknown keys) so it catches typos. See docs/PROTOCOL-DSL.md for the field reference.

_note.description: Free-text annotation ignored by the engine (JSON has no comments). Allowed on any object in a protocol.

name.description: Protocol id. Becomes the state-path prefix <name>/<instance-key>.yaml. Unique per protocols directory.

version.description: Protocol version (informational; not enforced by the engine).

min_engine_version.description: Lowest engine version this protocol requires. The dist installer refuses to install onto an older engine.

max_depth.description: Static nesting-depth cap for the protocol tree (default 5). The engine rejects a protocol whose deepest leaf path exceeds this.

phase_labels.description: Optional overrides for the PR labels of terminal/structural phases (e.g. setup, done, failed, blocked).

triggers.description: Event -> command entry points. The router scans every protocol's triggers to select one at runtime.

states.description: The protocol's top-level sequence of nodes (the root is a sequence).

_note.description: Free-text annotation; ignored by the engine.

on.description: GitHub event name, e.g. issue_comment or pull_request.

comment_prefix.description: For issue_comment: match comments starting with this prefix (e.g. /review). Also names the command's reply prefix (e.g. /answer).

actions.description: For pull_request: only fire on these actions (e.g. ["opened"]). Empty/omitted matches any.

check.description: A deterministic check over the agent's evidence. ABI: <check> <evidence.json> <diff.txt> <changed-files.txt> -> one JSON {check,pass,feedback}, always exit 0.

_note.description: Free-text annotation; ignored by the engine.

run.description: Check id. Resolved to checks/<run> or checks/<run>.* in the protocol dir (extension-agnostic) unless 'exec' is given.

exec.description: Explicit path (relative to the protocol dir) to the check executable, overriding the checks/<run> lookup.

on_fail.description: Severity when this check fails. iterate (default) drives the retry loop; block stops the conclusion without iterating; advisory is recorded only.

input.description: Materialize an earlier node's evidence (or a gate's answers) as inputs/<as>.json for this node's agent.

_note.description: Free-text annotation; ignored by the engine.

from.description: Source node id (a sibling sub-state, a fanout branch, or a phase).

as.description: Alias — the basename under inputs/ the source is materialized as.

node.description: One node in a sequence. The fields you may set depend on 'kind'.

_note.description: Free-text annotation; ignored by the engine.

id.description: Node id, unique within its enclosing sequence.

label.description: Human-readable label for the PR status comment / phase label (defaults to a humanized id).

next.description: Id of the sibling node to enter after this one completes. Omit on a terminal node; the terminals 'done' and 'failed' are implicit and need not be declared.

workflow.description: agent: the gh-aw agent workflow basename the engine dispatches.

evidence.description: agent: the *.evidence.schema.json filename (in the protocol dir) the agent must satisfy.

max_iterations.description: agent: max iterate-with-feedback rounds before the node is marked failed (default 3).

params.description: agent: node-scoped config forwarded to checks via the CHECK_PARAMS env var (e.g. the rubric categories).

checks.description: agent/gate: deterministic checks run over the evidence.

conclude.description: agent: a trusted (zone 4) post-checks hook that may return blocked=true.

publish.description: agent: a trusted (zone 4) hook that publishes the verdict after checks pass.

on_blocked.description: agent: behavior when 'conclude' returns blocked=true. 'halt' stops the pipeline (overridable via /override).

inputs.description: agent/gate/merge: earlier nodes' outputs to materialize for this node.

of.description: join: the id of the sibling fanout this join is an AND-barrier for.

questions_from.description: gate: makes this a DATA gate — names a sibling node whose evidence carries the questions to ask (answered via the command's /answer prefix). Omit for a human-APPROVAL gate.

approve_excludes_author.description: gate (approval): if true, the PR author cannot approve their own gate.

hook.description: merge: the trusted (zone 4) reduce hook that combines the joined legs' inputs.

branch.description: A fanout leg. Either a FLAT leg (has 'workflow') or a SUB-PIPELINE leg (has its own 'states'). A branch has no 'kind'.

_note.description: Free-text annotation; ignored by the engine.

id.description: Leg id, unique within the fanout.

workflow.description: Flat leg: the agent workflow basename.

states.description: Sub-pipeline leg: a nested sequence of nodes.
